text:
Celine Dionhas expressed her joy after making a triumphant return to live singing at the Paris 2024Olympicsopening ceremony. The Canadian songstress, 56, delivered a powerful rendition of Edith Piaf's L'Hymne A L'Amour from the heart of the Eiffel Tower on Friday, despite her ongoing battle with stiff person syndrome (SPS).

In 2021, Dion had to cancel her Las Vegas residency due to health issues and later revealed her SPS diagnosis in December 2022, leading to the cancellation of her Courage World Tour. This marked her first public performance since then.

Taking toInstagram, she wrote: "I'm honoured to have performed tonight, for the Paris 2024 Opening Ceremony, and so full of joy to be back in one of my very favourite cities! Most of all, I'm so happy to be celebrating these amazing athletes, with all their stories of sacrifice and determination, pain and perseverance.

"All of you have been so focused on your dream, and whether or not you take home a medal, I hope that being here means that it has come true for you! You should all be so proud, we know how hard you have worked to be the best of the best. Stay focused, keep going, my heart is with you!"

The five-time Grammy winner also shared pictures of herself making a heart sign, donned in the same beaded silver gown with a high neckline that she wore during her performance, along with an image of fans carrying a flag, designed in the Olympics-style with the "o" in her name written using the games' rings.

READ MORE:Lady Gaga's emotional Prince William and Princess Kate connection exposed



Celine Dion opened up about her rare condition, which leads to progressive muscular inflexibility, in the 2024 documentary 'I Am: Celine Dion'.

The programme featured extended footage of Dion experiencing an SPS attack before a performance, highlighting her determination to return to the stage.

In a surprising turn of events at the Paris opening ceremony, American pop icon Lady Gaga delighted viewers by performing the French classic 'Mon Truc En Plumes', emerging from pink feathered pom poms and donning a black bodice with a detachable black and pink ruffled skirt.

Don't miss:Celine Dion's comeback performance at Olympics leaves BBC viewers in tears

Don't miss:I'm a Celine Dion fan and her emotional Olympics performance can't be topped

Don't miss:Celine Dion sparks concern as worried Olympics viewers all have same question



Expressing her gratitude on X, formerly known as Twitter, she said: "I feel so completely grateful to have been asked to open the Paris Olympics 2024 this year."

"I am also humbled to be asked by the Olympics organising committee to sing such a special French song - a song to honour the French people and their tremendous history of art, music, and theatre."

"This song was sung by Zizi Jeanmaire, born in Paris a French ballerina, she famously sang Mon Truc En Plumes in 1961. The title means My Thing With Feathers."

Gaga further explained that it wasn't her first encounter with Jeanmaire's work, as the French ballerina starred in Cole Porter's musical 'Anything Goes', a jazz song Gaga released with Tony Bennett.

She concluded: "Although I am not a French artist, I have always felt a very special connection with French people and singing French music."

"I wanted nothing more than to create a performance that would warm the heart of France, celebrate French art and music, and on such a momentous occasion remind everyone of one of the most magical cities on earth - Paris. We rented pom poms from Le Lido archive - a real French cabaret theatre."

We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

"We collaborated with Dior to create custom costumes, using naturally moulted feathers. I studied French choreography that put a modern twist on a French classic. I rehearsed tirelessly to study a joyful French dance, brushing up on some old skills - I bet you didn't know I used to dance at a 60s French party on the Lower East Side when I was first starting out."

"I hope you love this performance as much as I do. And to everyone in France, thank you so much for welcoming me to your country to sing in honour of you - it's a gift I'll never forget."

title: Celine Dion issues four-word message after emotional Paris Olympics performance | Celebrity News | Showbiz & TV | Express.co.uk
author: Rebecca Jones
description: Music icon Celine Dion took the world's breath away when she performed in the heart of Paris for the Olympics Opening Ceremony - and the star has now issued a four-word response to her singing comeback.
